canonical_definition: A dataset of simple stories, which is a reproduction of TinyStories, used for training and evaluating language models in a controlled setting.

definitions.text: A dataset of simple stories, which is a reproduction of TinyStories, used for training and evaluating language models in a controlled setting.
A tiny-stories corpus generated with Mixtral-8x7B and used to train and validate language models on short-story continuation.

relationships.evidence: Our theoretical results are empirically confirmed with experiments in: ... • Realistic setting of GPT-2 models trained on BabiStories (Zhang et al., 2024a) (Section 1.1)

The TINYSTORIES work of Eldan & Li (2023) shows how to study large language modeling questions using small language models... we leverage... to generate a new corpus of tiny stories dubbed BABISTORIES.

characterization: A dataset of simple stories, which is a reproduction of TinyStories, used for training and evaluating language models in a controlled setting.
